name: Tomte
description: The Tomte, also known as Nisse in Norway and Denmark, is a solitary, mythological creature deeply rooted in Scandinavian folklore. He is typically depicted as a small, bearded man, often no taller than three feet, wearing a red cap and rustic clothing. The Tomte is a guardian spirit, watching over the farm and its inhabitants. His primary role is to ensure the prosperity and well-being of the farm. He is believed to be incredibly diligent and hard-working, taking care of livestock, mending fences, and generally assisting with the day-to-day tasks of farm life. However, the Tomte is also known for his temperamental nature. If treated with respect and offered regular gifts of porridge with butter, he will continue to protect and aid the farm. But if neglected, insulted, or treated poorly, he can become mischievous and even vengeful, causing misfortune and havoc around the farm. The Tomte represents the importance of respecting tradition and maintaining a harmonious relationship with the natural world.
subtitle: Guardian spirit of the Scandinavian farm
appearance: A small, bearded man wearing a red cap and rustic, often grey or brown clothing. He is typically depicted as being no taller than three feet. He may appear old and wizened, reflecting his ancient origins and connection to the land.
habitat: Traditional Scandinavian farms, barns, and outbuildings. He is typically associated with rural areas and places where agriculture is practiced.
behavior: Helpful and protective when treated well, but mischievous and vengeful when neglected or insulted. He is solitary and prefers to work unseen. He demands respect for tradition and proper treatment of the farm.
weaknesses: Lack of offerings (especially porridge with butter)
Exposure of his true name (if known)

keyMyths.title: The Tomte and the Christmas Porridge
keyMyths.description: One of the most well-known stories involves the Tomte's demand for Christmas porridge with butter. If the porridge is not provided, or if the butter is missing, the Tomte will become angry and cause mischief, such as tangling the animals' manes or hiding tools. This story emphasizes the importance of respecting the Tomte and fulfilling his expectations to maintain a harmonious relationship.

keyMyths.title: The Tomte's Revenge
keyMyths.description: Tales abound of Tomtes who were insulted or neglected and subsequently wreaked havoc on the farm. These stories often involve livestock falling ill, tools mysteriously disappearing, or the farm experiencing a string of bad luck. These tales serve as a cautionary reminder of the consequences of disrespecting the Tomte and the importance of maintaining a positive relationship with the guardian spirit.

keyMyths.title: The Tomte and the New Clothes
keyMyths.description: In some stories, a farmer attempts to give the Tomte new clothes, believing his old rags are unsuitable. However, the Tomte, considering this an insult, believing the farmer thinks he is not doing a good job, leaves the farm, taking its good fortune with him. This highlights the Tomte's pride and his belief that he is already providing valuable service to the farm.
